svg.text: Digital literacy essentials
Five core skills every B.Tech student needs to master
What is digital literacy and why it matters
Ability to find, use, and evaluate digital information effectively
Skills to stay safe, think critically, and communicate online
Foundation for every modern academic and professional career
Essential digital tools for B.Tech students
Google Workspace — Docs, Sheets, Slides, Forms, and Drive
GitHub (code), Canva (design), HackerRank (coding practice)
VS Code, Zoom, LinkedIn, Kaggle — your essential student toolkit
Safe internet practices & password hygiene
Use 8+ character passwords — mix letters, numbers, and symbols
Enable Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) on every account
Avoid phishing — always verify links and the sender's identity
Professional online presence (GitHub & LinkedIn)
GitHub — host your projects and write a profile README
LinkedIn — add your education, skills, and certifications
Keep all profiles professional, consistent, and regularly updated
Email etiquette in academic & professional life
Always use a clear, specific subject line for every email
Formal greeting, structured body, and a polite sign-off
Proofread before sending — tone matters as much as content
